items.headline: City Council reviews $2027 capital spending plans
items.summary: The council held a study session on the Fiscal Year 2026-27 Capital Improvement Program, which outlines major infrastructure and facility projects the city plans to fund. This is worth tracking if you care about what physical improvements (parks, roads, buildings, utilities) Mountain View will prioritize in the coming year.

items.headline: City Council Considers Changes to Tenant Protection Rules
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission introduced an ordinance to amend Sunnyvale's Residential Tenant Protections Program on April 14. Details on what specific protections are being added or modified were not provided in the agenda.

items.headline: Downtown building seeks historic status upgrade and bonus floor space
items.summary: The owner of 405 Kipling Street is requesting to elevate the building's historic designation from Category 4 to Category 2, which would allow an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area as an incentive for historic and seismic improvements. This could enable substantial expansion of a downtown property.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A proposal to rezone and redevelop 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road would replace commercial buildings with an 8-story mixed-use building containing 167 apartments (including 28 affordable units) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. This represents significant new housing and a major physical transformation of that corridor.

items.headline: City examines alternative housing programs in Housing Element update
items.summary: Council is reviewing Housing Element Program 6.5 on alternative housing options, signaling potential new approaches to addressing the city's housing needs beyond traditional single-family and apartment development.

items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing postponed; 333 Franklin Street project delayed
items.summary: The Administrative Zoning hearing scheduled for April 8, 2026 was cancelled, pushing back a project at 333 Franklin Street to a future date. The project will be re-noticed once a new hearing date is confirmed, likely affecting the timeline for this development.

items.headline: City Council authorizes construction contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the agenda document does not specify what project this contract covers. Residents may want to follow up on what construction work is being undertaken in Los Gatos.

items.headline: City awarded contract for local street pavement repairs across San José
items.summary: The City Council approved a contract for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project on April 7. This project will repair and replace pavement on local streets throughout San José, affecting neighborhood conditions and traffic.
